# End-to-end prune flow: create state entries, delete their kubeconfig files,
# ensure `kcm prune` reports them and --yes actually removes them.

mkdir $WORK/kube
cp $WORK/prod.yaml $WORK/kube/prod.yaml
cp $WORK/staging.yaml $WORK/kube/staging.yaml

# Seed state for both files.
exec kcm tag palette add prod staging
exec kcm tag add prod prod --dir $WORK/kube
exec kcm tag add staging staging --dir $WORK/kube

# No pruning needed yet.
exec kcm prune --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'no stale state entries found'

# Delete one of the kubeconfigs; its state entry is now stale.
rm $WORK/kube/staging.yaml

# Dry run lists it and exits without mutation.
exec kcm prune --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'Found 1 stale entry'
stdout 'staging.yaml'
stdout 'Run `kcm prune --yes`'

# Re-run confirms nothing was removed by the dry run.
exec kcm prune --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'Found 1 stale entry'

# Actually remove it.
exec kcm prune --dir $WORK/kube --yes
stdout 'Removed 1 stale entry'

# Idempotent: second --yes finds nothing.
exec kcm prune --dir $WORK/kube --yes
stdout 'no stale state entries found'

# The remaining prod entry is unaffected.
exec kcm tag list prod --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'prod.yaml'
stdout 'prod'

-- prod.yaml --
apiVersion: v1
kind: Config
current-context: prod-eu
clusters:
  - name: prod-eu-cluster
    cluster:
      server: https://prod-eu.example.test
contexts:
  - name: prod-eu
    context:
      cluster: prod-eu-cluster
      user: prod-admin
users:
  - name: prod-admin
    user:
      token: test
-- staging.yaml --
apiVersion: v1
kind: Config
current-context: staging
clusters:
  - name: staging-cluster
    cluster:
      server: https://staging.example.test
contexts:
  - name: staging
    context:
      cluster: staging-cluster
      user: staging-admin
users:
  - name: staging-admin
    user:
      token: test
